# Design System: Clean Shopper
**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** 2026-04-16
**Source:** Generated by /design-system-generator skill from brand interview

---

## How to Use This Document
This file is referenced by CLAUDE.md and read by Claude Code at the start of every session. Apply these tokens for all UI work. Do not hardcode hex values, pixel sizes, or spacing values in components. Reference token names from tailwind.config.js instead.

---

## Brand Direction
Clean Shopper feels light, sustainable, and transparent — like a trusted friend who helps you read the label. The visual language draws from earthy, muted tones inspired by Osea's palette and Coyuchi's generous, editorial spacing. Interactive elements carry the quiet confidence of deep forest greens, warm amber highlights echo optimism without noise, and the overall impression is calm and unhurried — the deliberate opposite of loud, cluttered wellness retail.

---

## Color System

### Primary Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| primary | #9E968E | Warm greige stone — used for primary text-on-light backgrounds, logo mark, and brand-level UI moments. Not used for interactive elements or decorative fills. |
| primary-light | #BDB6AF | Lighter stone — used for dividers with warm tone, inactive tab indicators, and muted icon fills. |
| primary-dark | #6B635B | Deeper stone — used for strong secondary text, input borders on focus, and supporting headings that need contrast without aggression. |

### Secondary Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| secondary-cream | #F2EDE4 | Soft earth cream — used for page section backgrounds, card surface fills, and input field backgrounds. The primary surface color throughout the app. |
| secondary-sage | #C5D5C9 | Light sea green — used for category tag backgrounds, ingredient-safe indicator backgrounds, and subtle hover fills on list items. Not used for text. |

### Accent Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| accent | #3D6147 | Forest green — used for primary buttons, active navigation states, selected states, links, and all primary call-to-action elements. One prominent use per view. Not used for backgrounds or decorative fills. |
| accent-light | #5A8268 | Lighter forest green — used for hover states on accent elements, secondary action buttons, and icon strokes on interactive components. |
| highlight | #D4AA5F | Sun amber gold — used for highlights, featured badges, "new" tags, star ratings, and warm emphasis moments. Not used for text or interactive controls. |

### Semantic Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| success | #4A7C5F | Positive states, "Clean" product ratings, confirmed saves, and completion states. Carries meaning — do not use for decoration. |
| warning | #C49A3C | Caution states, "Mixed" or partial ingredient ratings, alerts that need attention but are not errors. |
| error | #B85C4A | Error states, "Avoid" product ratings, destructive action confirmations, and form validation failures. Muted terracotta — not a harsh red. |

### Neutral Colors
| Token Name | Hex Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| neutral-50 | #FAF8F5 | Warm white — default page background for all views. |
| neutral-100 | #F2EDE4 | Cream — card backgrounds, surface fills, input backgrounds. Same as secondary-cream for consistency. |
| neutral-200 | #DDD7CE | Borders, dividers, horizontal rules, and card outlines. |
| neutral-400 | #A89E95 | Placeholder text, disabled element text, and inactive icon fills. |
| neutral-600 | #6B635B | Secondary text, supporting copy, metadata, and captions. Same tone as primary-dark. |
| neutral-900 | #2C2420 | Primary text and headings. Warm dark brown — never use pure black (#000000). |

---

## Typography

**Font Family (Display/Editorial):** Cormorant Garamond — a refined transitional serif that brings editorial warmth and quiet elegance to headlines and card labels, echoing Coyuchi's approach to typographic personality.
**Font Family (UI/Navigation):** Jost — a clean geometric sans-serif with subtle warmth, used for all interface-level text where clarity and utility matter most.
**Source:** Google Fonts

### Type Scale
| Token Name | Size | Weight | Line Height | Font | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| display | 56px | 500 | 1.1 | Cormorant Garamond | Hero headlines, marketing moments, landing page openers. Sparse use only. |
| h1 | 40px | 500 | 1.2 | Cormorant Garamond | Page titles — one per view. |
| h2 | 32px | 500 | 1.25 | Cormorant Garamond | Section headers that introduce a new content group on a page. |
| h3 | 24px | 500 | 1.3 | Cormorant Garamond | Card titles, product names, subsection headers within a content group. |
| h4 | 18px | 500 | 1.4 | Jost | Category labels, form section headers, small named groupings. |
| body | 16px | 400 | 1.65 | Jost | Body copy, product descriptions, ingredient explanations, all paragraph text. |
| small | 14px | 400 | 1.5 | Jost | Supporting text, metadata, tags, navigation items, button labels. |
| micro | 12px | 400 | 1.4 | Jost | Timestamps, legal text, tooltips, minimal UI labels. |

---

## Spacing Scale

Base unit: 8px

| Token Name | Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| space-xs | 4px | Icon padding, tight internal gaps between inline elements like tags. |
| space-sm | 8px | Compact internal padding within small components like badges and chips. |
| space-md | 16px | Standard internal padding inside buttons, inputs, and small cards. |
| space-lg | 24px | Card padding, list item padding, and internal spacing inside larger components. |
| space-xl | 40px | Vertical gap between components within a section. |
| space-2xl | 64px | Gap between major content sections on a page. |
| space-3xl | 96px | Page-level top/bottom margins and large section separators. |
| space-4xl | 128px | Hero sections, full-bleed area padding, and generous page-entry spacing. |

---

## Border Radius

| Token Name | Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| radius-sm | 4px | Badges, ingredient tags, small status chips, and tight inline labels. |
| radius-md | 8px | Input fields, buttons, small card thumbnails. |
| radius-lg | 16px | Product cards, modals, drawer panels, and larger container surfaces. |
| radius-full | 9999px | Pill-shaped tags, avatar circles, fully rounded icon buttons. |

---

## Shadows

| Token Name | Value | Usage Rule |
|---|---|---|
| shadow-sm | 0 1px 3px rgba(44, 36, 32, 0.06) | Resting card lift — subtle depth on product cards and surface panels at rest. |
| shadow-md | 0 4px 12px rgba(44, 36, 32, 0.08) | Hover state elevation on cards, open dropdown menus, and focused input fields. |
| shadow-lg | 0 8px 32px rgba(44, 36, 32, 0.12) | Modals, drawer overlays, and elements that float above the primary content layer. |

---

## Usage Rules Summary

These rules apply to every component Claude Code builds for this project:

1. Never hardcode a hex value. Use the token name from tailwind.config.js.
2. Never hardcode a pixel font size. Use the type scale token classes.
3. Never hardcode spacing. Use the spacing scale token classes.
4. Accent (forest green) is for the single most important interactive element per view — primary buttons, active states, and links. Not used for backgrounds, decoration, or secondary actions.
5. Neutral-900 for primary text. Neutral-600 for secondary text. Never use pure black (#000000) anywhere.
6. Semantic colors carry meaning. Do not use success green for anything other than positive ingredient or product safety states. Do not use error red for anything other than negative safety ratings, errors, or destructive actions.
7. Surfaces must use neutral-50 or secondary-cream. No bright white backgrounds (#FFFFFF).
8. Cormorant Garamond for display, h1, h2, h3 — editorial and product-facing text. Jost for h4, body, small, micro — interface and utility text.
9. Avoid saturated colors, loud gradients, and decorative patterns. If a background needs texture, use neutral tone-on-tone at very low opacity only.
10. Spacing should feel generous. When in doubt, add more vertical space — the design breathes like Coyuchi, not like a dashboard.
